data.narrative:
Reverion, a Series B climate and energy technology company, is valued at $310M post-money following $79M in total capital raised. The valuation assessment draws on a peer universe of nine comparable companies: one public entity (LNZA) and eight private firms across the same sector and development stage, including Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, CarbonCapture Inc., and Cemvita Factory.

Two valuation methods triangulate a defensible range. The stage-progression method applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the current $310M post-money valuation, implying $589M. The peer-median approach examines five same-sector, same-stage comparables with valuations ranging from $120M to $5.0B; the median across this set is $400M. The peer set demonstrates material dispersion, with Commonwealth Fusion Systems at the upper extreme ($5.0B) and Cemvita Factory at the lower bound ($120M).

Synthesizing these two methods produces a triangulated valuation range of $340M to $677M, with a median of $494M. The current $310M valuation sits below both implied endpoints, suggesting room for step-up in a subsequent financing. Key uncertainties include the timing and quantum of commercial traction relative to peer benchmarks, and the sustained capital intensity required to reach Series C inflection points typical of the climate-tech sector.
